UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501,COURT CLARIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING. ORAL ARGUMENTS ARE HEARD FROM BOTH SIDES. COURT HEARS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO SUPPLEMENT COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT OPPOSES THE REQUEST. REQUEST TO SUPPLEMENT COMPLAINT IS DENIED. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVINGS HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE AN ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT OR A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL, TO ALLEGE DUTY AND TO ALLEGE INJURY IN FACT. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK B. PAREDES, NOT REPORTED. (501)

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A.,AND RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING PLAINTIFFS' INJURY-IN-FACT AND HOW AND WHY DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS WERE UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR, OR FRAUDULENT WITHIN THE MEANING OF SECTION 17200. AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING STANDING TO ENFORCE THE CONTRACT AND WHAT SPECIFIC PROVISIONS OF THE CONTRACT WERE BREACHED BY THE FACTS ALLEGED. AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING CANCELLATION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEDND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE CIVIL CODE SECTION 2923.55 WAS NOT EFFECTIVE WHEN THE NOTICE OF DEFAULT WAS FILED AGAINST PLAINTIFF. PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PETER J BUSCH, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A. AND THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT OR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. SEE EXHIBITS TO COMPLAINT. DODD V. CITIZENS BANK OF COSTS MESA (1990) 222 CAL.APP.1624. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE STANDING TO ASSERT CANCELLATION OF INSTRUMENTS CAUSE OF ACTION. KESHTGAR V. U.S. BANK (2014) 226 CAL.APP.4TH 1201. DEMURER TO WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED, PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED FORECLOSURE IN VIOLATION OF COURT'S ORDER AND DAMAGES INCURRED AS A RESULT OF SUCH FORECLOSURE BEFORE THE RESCISSION. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE LILLIAN K. SING, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

No opposition filed. The Court shall release $2,500 plus interest, if any, to the Plaintiff. $2,500 deposited with the Court on February 4, 2013, TAG # 031509. Moving party to provide an order. Off Calendar Nov-19-2014 Continued To Sep-14-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Payal Cadman, Esq. of ReedSmith LLP, (415-543-8700), for Bank of America, Defendant and moving party.
No appearance by plaintiff and opposing party.

Court hears oral arguments by defendant. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA N.A. and RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Alvarez v. BAC Home Loans (2014) recognizes a "general duty" "to use reasonable care in the processing of a loan modification". Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT

DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA, RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS, THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLONS' NEW DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to March 9, 2015. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Set For Trial On Mar-16-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Continued To Sep-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Adrian L. Canzoneri, Esq. of ReedSmith, (415-659-4744), for Bank of America, Recontrust Company and The Bank of New York Mellon, Defendants and moving parties.
Michael Rooney, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415-772-9875), for Jovica Maksimovic, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as as follows: DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A. and THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first and third cause of action. Plaintiff fails to allege a cause of action for breach of contract or promissory estoppel. See Exhibits to Complaint. Dodd v. Citizens Bank of Costs Mesa (1990) 222 Cal.App.1624. Plaintiff does not have standing to assert cancellation of instruments cause of action. Keshtgar v. U.S. Bank (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1201. Demurer to wrongful foreclosure cause of action is overruled, Plaintiff sufficiently alleged foreclosure in violation of Court's order and damages incurred as a result of such foreclosure before the rescission. Order is signed in open court. Judge Lillian K. Sing, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Salaysay, Esq, attorney at law, (415-470-9018), specially appearing for Jovica Maksimovic Plaintiff and opposing party.
Shaudee Navid, Esq, attorney at law, (415-659-5674), for Bank of America N.A. Reconstrust Co., N.A. Defendant and moving party.

Court clarifies the tentative ruling. Oral arguments are heard from both sides. Court hears plaintiff's request to supplement complaint. Defendant opposes the request. Request to supplement complaint is denied. Court having reviewed the pleadings and havings heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege an enforceable contract or a cause of action for promissory estoppel, to allege duty and to allege injury in fact. Order is signed in open Court. Judge A. James Robertson II, Clerk B. Paredes, Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty Shaudee Navid of Reed Smith LLP (415-659-5674) appear for defendant Bank of America N.A. and Recontrust Company, N.A. moving party. No other appearance.

Tentative ruling is adopted, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A.,and RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND as to the first, second, third, fifth, and sixth causes of action. As to the first and fifth causes of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts showing Plaintiffs' injury-in-fact and how and why Defendants' actions were unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent within the meaning of section 17200. As to the second and third causes of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts supporting standing to enforce the contract and what specific provisions of the contract were breached by the facts alleged. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts supporting cancellation. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEDND as to the fourth cause of action because Civil Code section 2923.55 was not effective when the Notice of Default was filed against Plaintiff. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Peter J Busch, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)
